title: fix: tests webserver syntaxwarning
body:
> Changed in Python version 3.12: Unrecognized escape sequences produce a SyntaxWarning. In a future Python version they will be eventually a SyntaxError.
[2.4.1.1. Escape sequences](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#index-23)

Change to raw string literal to fix:
```
/webserver.py:17: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  elif re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path):
/webserver.py:18: SyntaxWarning: invalid escape sequence '\d'
  result = re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path)
```

step_plan.summary: Convert the regex pattern literals in Handler.do_GET of the test webserver to raw string literals (r"/status/(\d+)") to eliminate Python 3.12 SyntaxWarning for invalid escape sequences.

steps.operation: Replace non-raw regex pattern literals with raw string literals in test webserver
steps.reason: The method contains two calls to re.match("/status/(\d+)", self.path) where the pattern uses backslashes. In Python 3.12 unrecognized escape sequences (like \d in a normal string) produce a SyntaxWarning and will become a SyntaxError in the future. Converting the pattern strings to raw string literals (r"/status/(\d+)") prevents Python from treating \d as an escape sequence while preserving the regex semantics.
steps.side_effects: No behavioral change to the webserver matching logic; fully backwards compatible. Run the test suite to ensure no tests rely on exact string quoting. Downstream code that depends on Handler.do_GET will see no API change. This change only addresses warnings; it does not alter regex semantics. If other similar regex literals exist elsewhere, they will still emit warnings and should be updated separately.
